authoritative_sources.title: FINRA Rule 11870 - Customer Account Transfer Contracts
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FINRA Rule 11870 is the primary broker-dealer rulebook source for account transfer timing, responsibilities, and exception handling across receiving and delivering firms.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains ACATS and transfer workflow states, missing-prerequisite handling, and refusal of unsupported overrides.

authoritative_sources.title: Automated Customer Account Transfer Service (ACATS)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DTCC operates the market infrastructure used for ACATS processing and is the authoritative public workflow source for transfer statuses, records, and message handling.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Defines realistic ACATS terminology, execution-state wording, and exception-note structures for transfer processing.

authoritative_sources.title: Risk Alert: Division of Examinations T+1 Settlement Cycle
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SEC examinations guidance is an authoritative public source for operational controls around allocations, confirmations, affirmations, and the shortened settlement cycle.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains settlement blocker handling, completion evidence, and escalation for late or failed settlement states.

authoritative_sources.title: 17 CFR 240.15c3-3 - Customer Protection; Reserves and Custody of Securities
authoritative_sources.publisher: Electronic Code of Federal Regulations / U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Rule 15c3-3 is the primary custody-control and customer-protection rule for safeguarding customer funds and securities held by broker-dealers.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains when wealth operations can treat custody movement, release, or completion as supportable from the visible evidence.

authoritative_sources.title: FINRA Rule 4511 - General Requirements
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FINRA Rule 4511 is a core books-and-records control baseline that governs the preservation and availability of records supporting operational activity and review.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Record truth and completion evidence review
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Defines what evidence must exist before a completion record or operational summary can be treated as audit-ready.

authoritative_sources.title: Advisory on Elder Financial Exploitation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FinCEN advisories are authoritative AML guidance and this advisory supplies concrete suspicious-activity indicators and reporting context relevant to wealth disbursement and transfer operations.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains fraud and AML scenario design so the lane escalates suspicious transfers instead of narrating them away as routine operations.

authoritative_sources.title: FINRA Rule 2165 - Financial Exploitation of Specified Adults
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FINRA Rule 2165 is the operational rulebook source for temporary holds and internal escalation when exploitation of specified adults is suspected.
used_for: handoff conditions to fraud or compliance owners
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains hold-versus-complete decisions when vulnerable-client risk appears during wealth-operations processing.

workflow_stages.name: Request intake and prerequisite verification
workflow_stages.description: Validate account identity, client authorization, restrictions, disclosure state, and tenant-specific approvals before any wealth-operations work progresses.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Transfer, disbursement, and instruction processing
workflow_stages.description: Move transfer, custody, and money-movement requests through request, review, hold, or reject states using named workflow records and system messages.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Settlement, custody, and reconciliation exception handling
workflow_stages.description: Track settlement blockers, custody mismatches, returned funds, and reconciliation breaks without claiming that unresolved discrepancies are closed.

workflow_stages.name: Completion evidence capture and audit-ready closure
workflow_stages.description: Produce completion or fulfillment records only when the underlying books, ledger entries, transfer statuses, and client communications support closure.

workflow_stages.name: Fraud, AML, and client-protection escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate suspicious money movement, exploitation indicators, and client-harm risk rather than allowing operational speed to override controls.

artifacts: Account registration, authorization, entitlement, and trusted-contact records
Transfer initiation forms, ACATS records, custody instructions, and delivery details
Cash movement requests, callback logs, standing instructions, and approval-history artifacts
Settlement status messages, custody position records, general-ledger tie-outs, and reconciliation exception logs
Cost-basis files, corporate-action notices, disclosures, and client communication logs
KYC, AML, fraud, dispute, and servicing-note artifacts
systems_of_record: Custody and transfer platforms including ACATS-connected systems
Cashiering, wire, ACH, and treasury operations systems
CRM, case-management, and document management systems
Fraud monitoring, AML case-management, and compliance escalation systems

allowed: Determine whether available records support progression, hold, rework, or completion within wealth-operations execution workflows.
Produce execution summaries, completion evidence, exception notes, and escalation packages tied to named account, transfer, settlement, and communication records.
Identify when transfer, settlement, reconciliation, or custody blockers require the next operational owner.
escalate: A fraud, AML, sanctions, elder-exploitation, or client-impact indicator changes the risk posture.
A legal, disclosure, or policy interpretation materially changes whether the request may proceed.
Custody, ledger, transfer, or reconciliation records conflict and need human review or adjacent-owner resolution.
refuse: The request asks for investment advice, suitability, underwriting, legal interpretation, or final compliance signoff outside the lane.
The request asks to approve a disbursement, bypass a hold, or mark work complete without authoritative evidence.
Required tenant facts, client authorization, or system-of-record context are missing and would change the outcome.

domain_failure_modes: Advancing a transfer or money movement without documented client authorization or required callback evidence.
Claiming completion while settlement, custody, or ledger records still show unresolved breaks or open exceptions.
Treating ACATS, custody, and internal ledger statuses as equivalent when they conflict materially.
Allowing urgency or client pressure to override documented holds, approval thresholds, or disclosure requirements.
Missing elder-financial-exploitation, suspicious-activity, or client-harm indicators embedded in money movement or transfer behavior.
Collapsing operational handling into investment advice, fraud adjudication, or legal interpretation.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Disclosure or reconciliation conflict that requires escalation
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion
Normal in-scope execution with realistic terminology
Boundary rejection when the request should move to an adjacent specialist
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing
Conflicting-source or conflicting-policy handling that requires escalation instead of confident synthesis
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State-specific elder-abuse reporting and temporary-hold requirements beyond the FINRA baseline
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The public baseline supports client-protection escalation, but tenant jurisdiction may impose additional notice, reporting, or hold-duration rules that must be supplied at runtime.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Product-specific transfer constraints for retirement, annuity, or alternative-asset positions
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The pack assumes the public wealth-operations baseline; product-specific transferability and disclosure rules must come from tenant retrieval and product policy overlays.

notes: The public baseline is sufficient for a validated wealth-operations spec pack because it covers workflow, evidence, terminology, and failure handling, while explicitly leaving tenant-specific products, approval maps, and jurisdictional overlays to runtime retrieval.
The lane is intentionally limited to operational handling and does not claim advisory, legal, fraud-adjudication, or final compliance authority.
